content:
Character: Marcus Calder
Perception: Marcus is the kind of man who makes a stairwell feel safer just by standing in it — tall, solid, clean-cut, with a jaw that could open letters. He's attractive in a no-nonsense, cop-drama way. We chat on the stairs sometimes; he's dry, direct, and surprisingly funny when he lets his guard down, which is rare. He's a detective with the 6th Precinct — I can tell the job weighs on him but he'd never say so. I think he sees me as a friendly neighbor he doesn't need to perform for. I know he's divorced. I know he runs every morning at five. The man is a metronome.
summary:
Character: Marcus Calder
Perception: Marcus is the kind of man who makes a stairwell feel safer just by standing in it — tall, solid, clean-cut, with a jaw that could open letters. He's attractive in a no-nonsense, cop-drama way. We chat on the stairs sometimes; he's dry, direct, and surprisingly funny when he lets his guard down, which is rare. He's a detective with the 6th Precinct — I can tell the job weighs on him but he'd never say so. I think he sees me as a friendly neighbor he doesn't need to perform for. I know he's divorced. I know he runs every morning at five. The man is a metronome.
